_note: Leaply Hokuto Message × Format Matrix snapshot — extracted from screenshots of hokuto.leaplyhub.com (account: A7_Leaply, last refresh 23.05.2026 09:00). 17 Core Messages × 7 Visual Formats = max 119 cells; 71 active. Descriptions written to help AI map new hooks into the right CM/VF/PP/HT.

core_messages.name: Story Base Movie
core_messages.description: Narrative-driven hook unfolding like a short film. Character transformation arc — slow emotional build, payoff moment near end. Often opens with mundane situation, reveals deeper truth via personal journey. Uses real-life storytelling rhythm: setup → tension → discovery.
examples: My mom carried this for 30 years before someone finally explained why.
She came in for back pain. She left with the answer she'd been chasing for 8 years.

core_messages.name: Kids View
core_messages.description: Reframes adult body issue through a child's innocent perspective. Kid observes / points out / asks about a parent's hump, posture, pain — exposing what adults rationalize away. High emotional resonance for parents-as-buyers.
examples: My daughter pointed at my neck and asked why I have a 'bump'.
Kids see the truth grown-ups have been ignoring for years.

core_messages.name: Pattern Interrupt
core_messages.description: Scroll-stopping unexpected opening. Visual jolt, contradiction, weird sound, unusual claim. Designed to break the autopilot scroll in the first 1-2 seconds. Often counter-intuitive framing.
examples: Wait. Did you just feel that crackle in your neck?
Don't keep scrolling if your shoulders are tight right now.

core_messages.name: Asian Approach
core_messages.description: Culture-specific framing leveraging Eastern medicine / TCM / ancient wisdom as authority. May feature Asian actors, imagery, references to longevity traditions. Positions Leaply technique as time-tested rather than 'new fad'.
examples: What Japanese grandmothers have done for 1,000 years.
The Chinese discovered this 3,000 years ago. We just forgot.

core_messages.name: Developmental Window
core_messages.description: Urgency around a critical age threshold — frames a window of opportunity that's closing. 'By 50, this is irreversible.' Combines age-anxiety with actionable now-or-never positioning.
examples: By 50, this neck hump is irreversible.
There's a 7-year window. Most people miss it.

core_messages.name: Revealing
core_messages.description: Exposes hidden truths the mainstream / medical establishment overlooks. 'What doctors don't tell you' / 'Insider knowledge' / 'The real reason'. Combines authority claim with conspiracy-light framing.
examples: Your doctor won't tell you this about morning swelling.
What physical therapists know but never explain.

core_messages.name: Success Story
core_messages.description: Testimonial-driven before/after with specific named individual + measurable result. Anchors credibility through tangible numbers, time periods, real transformations. Often UGC or staged-as-UGC format.
examples: I lost 5cm of neck swelling in 14 days. Here's what I did.
30 days in, my hump is barely visible. Real photos.

core_messages.name: Call-Out
core_messages.description: Direct, accusatory framing pointed at the viewer's specific behavior. 'You're sitting too long. Yes, YOU.' Confrontational tone forces self-recognition. High-tension hook, high stop-scroll.
examples: You sit 8 hours a day. Your lymph is screaming. Yes, YOU.
Stop pretending this neck pain is normal.

core_messages.name: The Urgent Appeal
core_messages.description: Emotional plea with implied deadline. Soft urgency through care-framing rather than fear. 'Please don't wait until you can't move' — protective, almost maternal tone.
examples: Please. Don't wait until you can't turn your head.
Do this for yourself before it becomes a real problem.

core_messages.name: Future Potential
core_messages.description: Promise of better-self if action taken now. Vision-casting: 'In 6 months you could be...'. Combines aspirational identity with concrete change pathway. Less fear, more possibility.
examples: Imagine waking up without that neck stiffness in 60 days.
Your 55-year-old self will thank you for starting today.

core_messages.name: Parenting Approach
core_messages.description: Frames Leaply through parental role — mom's stress / kid's posture / family wellbeing. Targets parent-as-buyer with empathetic 'you give to everyone except yourself' angle.
examples: Mom of 3 here. I forgot what 'no pain' feels like.
Your toddler isn't the only one having a meltdown — your nervous system is too.

core_messages.name: FOMO
core_messages.description: Social-proof + scarcity. '87% of women have this and don't know.' / 'Everyone is talking about this.' Leverages crowd validation + fear of being left out / behind.
examples: 87% of women over 35 have blocked lymph and don't know it.
Everyone in my office is doing this 5-min ritual. I was the last.

core_messages.name: Stoic Kid
core_messages.description: Child role-model framing — features a kid who handles discomfort, has good posture, sits straight, exhibits discipline. Inverts the parenting-approach: child shows the parent what's possible.
examples: My 8-year-old has better posture than I do. Here's why.
Watch how she sits. Adults forgot.

core_messages.name: Other
core_messages.description: Catchall for hooks that don't cleanly fit one of the 16 specific Core Messages. Use sparingly — only when no other CM is a reasonable match. Often experimental angles.
examples: Unconventional framing not covered by other CMs.
Cross-category mashups.

core_messages.name: Inside Look
core_messages.description: Behind-the-scenes / clinical insider perspective. Anatomical diagrams, mechanism explanation, 'here's exactly what's happening inside your body'. Combines education with authority. Less fear, more curiosity.
examples: Here's what your lymph actually does at 3am.
Inside view of why your shoulders hurt — anatomically.

core_messages.name: Time spent on dumb ways
core_messages.description: Calls out wasted resources / failed methods. '$4K on massages, $2K on PT, this fixes it in 30 days for $X.' Mocks alternatives the viewer has already tried. Cathartic for 'I've tried everything' audience.
examples: $4,000 on massages over 10 years. This took 30 days.
Foam roller, PT, chiro, expensive pillows — none worked. This did.

core_messages.name: Expert-Guided Transformation
core_messages.description: Doctor / PT / certified specialist leads viewer step-by-step through method. Authority + structured pathway. Less fear, more competence. Often features credentials, lab coat, clinical setting.
examples: Physiotherapist of 20 years walks you through the exact 5 steps.
Sports medicine doctor: here's the routine I give every patient.

pain_points.name: general
pain_points.description: Generic wellness / vague fatigue / 'something feels off' — not localized to a body part.

pain_points.name: legs
pain_points.description: Leg swelling, heavy legs, varicose feel, end-of-day fluid retention.

pain_points.name: plantar fasciitis
pain_points.description: Foot pain, especially first steps in the morning, sharp heel pain.

pain_points.name: face
pain_points.description: Facial puffiness, weak jawline, sagging cheeks, morning swelling.

pain_points.name: neck & shoulders
pain_points.description: Tension, stiffness, tension headaches from desk posture.

pain_points.name: neck hump
pain_points.description: Buffalo hump / dowager's hump, visible from side, postural curve at base of neck.

pain_points.name: back
pain_points.description: Lower back tightness, sitting-induced pain, glute and hip stiffness.

pain_points.name: armpit
pain_points.description: Tender lymph nodes, side-of-chest fullness, congestion under arms.

pain_points.name: stiffness & pain
pain_points.description: Morning stiffness, generalized body aches, takes a while to 'get going'.

pain_points.name: sciatic nerve
pain_points.description: Shooting / radiating pain from lower back down one leg.

pain_points.name: deep hips
pain_points.description: Hip flexor tightness, deep glute / piriformis tension, sitting-related hip pain.

visual_formats.name: Visual Evidence
visual_formats.description: Before/after splits, transformation reveals, data overlays, anatomical diagrams. Proof-driven composition where the visual IS the argument.

visual_formats.name: Text-Centric
visual_formats.description: Large overlaid typography drives the message; subject minimal or simple. Negative space designed for words. Single dominant claim style.

visual_formats.name: Action / Lifestyle
visual_formats.description: Subject mid-activity in daily-life context (walking, sitting at desk, cooking, picking up child). Environmental, dynamic, grounded in real moment.

visual_formats.name: Professional Authority
visual_formats.description: Doctor / PT / expert in clinical setting, lab coat, soft directional clinical light, direct camera gaze. Gravitas + clinical aesthetic.

visual_formats.name: Native / Raw UGC
visual_formats.description: Selfie-style, real-person raw feel, no studio polish, handheld camera angle, natural unfiltered lighting. Looks like organic Instagram post.

visual_formats.name: Social Interaction
visual_formats.description: Two people in a conversation moment — one teaching/advising/confiding to other. Candid intimate dynamic, soft indoor light.

visual_formats.name: Other
visual_formats.description: Catchall for hybrid / experimental visual formats outside the 6 main categories.

hook_types.name: Problem-reveal
hook_types.description: Names a hidden problem viewer didn't know they had. Diagnostic framing.

hook_types.name: Authority statement
hook_types.description: Expert delivers a definitive claim with full conviction. 'As a doctor of 20 years, I'm telling you...'

hook_types.name: Pattern interrupt
hook_types.description: Unexpected visual or statement to halt scroll. Counter-intuitive.

hook_types.name: Curiosity gap
hook_types.description: Promises a reveal — viewer must watch to find out. 'You won't believe what...' but tasteful.

hook_types.name: Fear trigger
hook_types.description: Health scare, urgency, irreversible consequence framing.

hook_types.name: Social proof
hook_types.description: Testimonial-style drop-in, 'everyone is doing X', crowd validation.

hook_types.name: Frankenstein
hook_types.description: Mash-up of multiple winning hook formats combined into one ad.
